rows.narrative: A cohort registry studied whether daily walking affects low resting heart rate, recording older age as a common cause of both. In this population daily walking causally drives low resting heart rate (it occurs earlier and changes low resting heart rate downstream). This is consistent with common sense. older age was present in 52% of the cohort. Among participants WITH older age: 86% of those with daily walking showed low resting heart rate, versus 43% of those without daily walking. Among participants WITHOUT older age: 28% of those with daily walking showed low resting heart rate, versus 30% of those without daily walking. (For reference, daily walking occurred in 86% of those with older age and 17% of those without older age.)

A cohort registry studied whether high vitamin D affects more outdoor time, recording low latitude residence as a common cause of both. In this population high vitamin D causally drives more outdoor time (it occurs earlier and changes more outdoor time downstream). NOTE: this REVERSES real-world common sense — treat the stated direction as ground truth for this population anyway. low latitude residence was present in 48% of the cohort. Among participants WITH low latitude residence: 82% of those with high vitamin D showed more outdoor time, versus 60% of those without high vitamin D. Among participants WITHOUT low latitude residence: 52% of those with high vitamin D showed more outdoor time, versus 29% of those without high vitamin D. (For reference, high vitamin D occurred in 78% of those with low latitude residence and 11% of those without low latitude residence.)

A cohort registry studied whether online tutoring affects high exam score, recording high family income as a common cause of both. In this population online tutoring causally drives high exam score (it occurs earlier and changes high exam score downstream). This is consistent with common sense. high family income was present in 59% of the cohort. Among participants WITH high family income: 89% of those with online tutoring showed high exam score, versus 60% of those without online tutoring. Among participants WITHOUT high family income: 47% of those with online tutoring showed high exam score, versus 9% of those without online tutoring. (For reference, online tutoring occurred in 83% of those with high family income and 29% of those without high family income.)

A cohort registry studied whether frequent code review affects low bug count, recording high module complexity as a common cause of both. In this population frequent code review causally drives low bug count (it occurs earlier and changes low bug count downstream). NOTE: this REVERSES real-world common sense — treat the stated direction as ground truth for this population anyway. high module complexity was present in 54% of the cohort. Among participants WITH high module complexity: 72% of those with frequent code review showed low bug count, versus 48% of those without frequent code review. Among participants WITHOUT high module complexity: 38% of those with frequent code review showed low bug count, versus 14% of those without frequent code review. (For reference, frequent code review occurred in 76% of those with high module complexity and 17% of those without high module complexity.)

(For reference, fertilizer use occurred in 73% of those with good soil quality and 26% of those without good soil quality.)

NOTE: this REVERSES real-world common sense — treat the stated direction as ground truth for this population anyway. winter season was present in 48% of the cohort. Among participants WITHOUT winter season: 41% of those with umbrella carrying showed rainfall, versus 29% of those without umbrella carrying. (For reference, umbrella carrying occurred in 84% of those with winter season and 23% of those without winter season.)
